description_long: This is a white and gray spaceplane-style airplane with a streamlined fuselage and sharply pointed nose, reminiscent of the Chinese Shenlong or similar orbital vehicles. The wings are delta-shaped and smoothly integrated into the body, with visible black markings and text on the upper surfaces. Two cylindrical blue-gray engine nacelles are mounted near the rear, flanking a set of three black rocket nozzles. The tail features a single vertical stabilizer adorned with a red flag and yellow star insignia. The landing gear is deployed, with two main wheels under the wings and a single nose wheel. The cockpit is forward-mounted, with a black canopy and several small windows.
description: A white and gray delta-winged spaceplane with blue-gray rear engines, a single vertical tail, and a forward black cockpit canopy.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, the single vertical tail with insignia, and the deployed landing gear.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the three black rocket nozzles, blue-gray engine nacelles, and the wide delta wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the landing gear and engine placement.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the pointed nose, black cockpit canopy, and the broad delta wing shape.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-mounted delta wings with no visible winglets, smoothly blended into the fuselage, exhibiting a straight leading edge and broad span.
attribute_engine_type: Three rear-mounted jet or rocket engines, with two cylindrical nacelles flanking a central nozzle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and no visible horizontal stabilizers extending beyond the delta wing.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units under the wings and a single nose gear, all deployed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a black canopy and multiple small windows, likely multi-seat.
